This PR refines the opportunity detail view and restructures the status grouping system:

- Reduce opportunity title font size from `heading-1` (32px) to `heading-2` (24px) for improved visual hierarchy
- Add max-width constraint (900px) to the overview tab for better readability
- Restructure display groups with clearer status hierarchy: adds new "in-progress" group, renames "needs-review" → "for-review", and "archive" → "archived"
- Update subsection labels for improved clarity

body:
## Description

Jira ticket: AMP-140638

**Context:** The Opportunity Manager previously handled all phases of opportunity discovery, investigation, and graph mutation in a single agent pass. This made it hard to parallelize deep-dives across different product categories and led to shallow investigations when the scope was broad. We want the manager to focus on strategic triage and dispatch, while dedicated investigator subagents perform deep, category-scoped research and directly mutate the opportunity graph.

This PR also reframes the Opportunity Hub as an **evolving semantic graph** of product progress — nodes are initiatives (themes, epics, workstreams) in various lifecycle states, and edges are typed semantic relations (`PARENT_OF`, `BLOCKS`, `DUPLICATE_OF`, etc.). Rather than always creating new issues, agents now search the graph first and update existing nodes when appropriate.

**What changed:** The Opportunity Manager now triages signals, groups them into themes, and dispatches parallel investigator subagents — each scoped to a single category — that perform deep research and mutate the opportunity graph directly. Investigators return compact structured responses (`touched_opportunity_ids`, `touched_relation_ids`) so the manager can synthesize across categories and deduplicate.

**Guarantee:** No runtime behavior changes for end users. These are agent flow configurations and internal model definitions only invoked programmatically by the opportunity pipeline. Existing Opportunity Hub data and API are unaffected.

### Architecture

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph Manager["Opportunity Manager (strategic triage)"]
        A[Broad signal collection] --> B[Theme grouping & triage]
        B --> C[Prepare investigator requests]
    end

    C -->|dispatch| D1[Investigator: Category A]
    C -->|dispatch| D2[Investigator: Category B]
    C -->|dispatch| D3[Investigator: Category N]

    subgraph Investigator["Investigator Subagent (deep dive)"]
        direction TB
        E[Search existing graph nodes] --> F[Verify current product state]
        F --> G[Parallel research: analytics, replays, feedback]
        G --> H{Create vs Update?}
        H -->|existing node| I[Update node + add evidence]
        H -->|genuinely new| J[Create new node + relations]
        I --> K[Return touched_opportunity_ids]
        J --> K
    end

    D1 --> Investigator
    D2 --> Investigator
    D3 --> Investigator

    K --> L[Manager: cross-category synthesis]
    L --> M[Deduplicate & reconcile overlaps]
    M --> N[Executive opportunity report]
```

### Changes

- **New models:** `OpportunityInvestigatorRequest` / `OpportunityInvestigatorResponse` — compact graph-centric I/O for manager↔investigator communication
- **Investigator rewrite:** graph-first investigation flow with product-state verification, PM create-vs-update heuristics, RICC quality gates
- **Manager rewrite:** refocused on broad research → theme triage → parallel dispatch → cross-category synthesis
- **Tool parity:** investigator now has full Opportunity Hub CRUD access (previously manager-only)
- **Removed:** `create_prd` and `documentation_question` tools from both flows
- **Tool descriptions:** `OpportunityHubTool` docstrings reframed as semantic graph node/edge/annotation operations
- **Mermaid guidance:** both agents encouraged to use fenced mermaid blocks for non-trivial graph structures

## Testing

Existing `FlowConfig` tests (`test_config_yamls.py`, `test_config.py`) validate YAML parsing and model path resolution. Custom model serialization checks confirm request/response round-trips — confidence: medium (config-only change, no runtime unit tests)

## Feature Flags & DynconfV2

**Added:** None
**Related:** None — agent flows are invoked programmatically, not gated by dynconf

## Deploy Strategy

**Blast radius:** Opportunity pipeline agent behavior only, no flag gate — no change to user-facing features
**Prod risk:** Low — configuration and prompt changes to an internal agent pipeline. The investigator subagent is hidden (`display_status: hidden`) and only invoked by the manager.

## Monitoring

Validated through integration runs of the opportunity pipeline. Success measured by:
- Investigator subagents returning well-formed structured responses
- Graph mutations (updates, new nodes, relations) matching investigation findings
- Reduction in duplicate or shallow opportunity nodes

## Security

No impact on Data Access Controls. Agents operate within existing tool permissions (Amplitude MCP, session replay, feedback, knowledge base). Safe for Demo org — no new data access patterns.

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes the internal opportunity pipeline flow to parallelize investigations and directly mutate the Opportunity Hub graph via a new subagent, which can affect opportunity/relations data if prompts or dispatch logic misbehave. No end-user runtime paths are touched, but the blast radius includes automated graph updates and deduping behavior.
> 
> **Overview**
> **Adds a new `Opportunity Investigator` flow** that receives a structured category request, performs targeted evidence gathering (analytics, replays, feedback, KB), and **updates/creates Opportunity Hub nodes and relations** with a graph-first, quality-gated (RICC + multi-source) workflow.
> 
> **Refactors `Opportunity Manager v2`** from single-agent deep dive to **broad research → theme triage → parallel investigator dispatch → synthesis/reporting**, and wires in the investigator flow as a tool dependency.
> 
> **Introduces structured manager↔investigator I/O models** (`OpportunityInvestigatorRequest`/`OpportunityInvestigatorResponse`) for compact reporting of touched opportunity/relation/evidence IDs, and updates `OpportunityHubTool` docstrings to emphasize semantic graph mutation conventions and safer usage patterns (search-before-create, tag/comment guidance).
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit e72897a5933681ee05e069c9238739bb824bf8ce. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

## Description

Refactors the opportunity detail view from an inline modal/drawer component within the opportunities list to a dedicated detail page accessible via routing. This improves navigation, allows direct linking to specific opportunities, and provides a cleaner separation of concerns.

**Key changes:**
- Created new `OpportunityDetailPage` component that serves as the detail view for a single opportunity
- Added route `/opportunities/:opportunityId` to display the detail page
- Removed inline detail view logic from `Opportunities` component - now clicking an opportunity card navigates to its detail page instead of opening a modal
- Updated `OpportunitiesPage` and `opportunities.tsx` entrypoint to no longer pass `relationsByOpportunityId` to the `Opportunities` component
- The detail page fetches its own opportunity and relations data via `useOpportunities` hook

This enables:
- Direct linking to specific opportunities via URL
- Better browser history/back button behavior
- Cleaner component responsibilities
- Easier testing of detail view in isolation

## Testing

Verified the following scenarios:
- Clicking an opportunity card navigates to `/opportunities/{id}` detail page
- Detail page displays opportunity title, status, source, and tags
- Back button returns to opportunities list
- "Opportunity not found" state displays when navigating to invalid ID
- All four tabs (Overview, Sources, Actions, Outcome) render without errors
- Existing opportunities list functionality remains unchanged

> Medium risk due to new routing/navigation paths and a new shared manifest loader that changes how microfrontend entries and CSS are loaded. UI semantics also shift (relations→sources) and badge styling changes may affect visual consistency.

> **Overview**
> **Opportunity detail is now a dedicated page route.** Clicking a card no longer toggles an inline drawer; `Opportunities` becomes a pure list that calls an `onSelectOpportunity` callback, and agents adds `/opportunities/:opportunityId` to render the new `OpportunityDetailPage` (with loading + not-found states and `Overview`/`Sources`/`Actions`/`Outcome` tabs).
> 
> **Lightning integration is updated for deep-linking and shared loading.** Lightning routes now accept `/opportunities/:opportunityId`, and a new shared `createManifestLoader` (`shared/src/onenav/load-manifest-entry.tsx`) replaces bespoke manifest/CSS injection, enabling `loadAgents.entry()` plus `loadAgents.namedEntry()` to load the list and the newly exported named detail component (`OpportunityDetail`).

> **UI/text/styling tweaks.** Status config now includes badge `variant`s and tags use `getTagBadgeVariant`; copy changes from *relations* to *sources* and i18n strings are added. Agents Vite config now uses `postcss.config.cjs` only in production instead of inline PostCSS plugins.

body: ## Description Addresses a regression where the "Save" and "Cancel" buttons in the channel classifier detail view were still being overlapped by the Floating Action Button (FAB). The previous fix provided insufficient right clearance (only 8px buffer), leading to overlap issues, especially with scrollbars. ## Description Implements React Error Boundaries around all artifact rendering components to prevent cascading JavaScript errors and provide a graceful recovery UI. ## Description This PR makes notification configuration optional during agent creation to reduce friction and improve conversion. Previously, users were required to configure Slack or email notifications, leading to significant time spent and abandonment. **Key changes include:** * **Optional Notifications:** Notification fields are no longer required for agent creation and default to empty. * **"Skip for now" Affordance:** A "Skip for now — you can add notifications after creating your agent" link is added to the notification section during agent creation. Clicking it collapses the section and clears any input. * **Post-Creation Nudge:** A dismissible banner "💡 Add Slack or email notifications to get daily insights delivered automatically" appears on the agent detail page if no notifications are configured, with an "Add notifications" CTA that opens the settings drawer. These changes address the blocking nature of notification setup, allowing users to create agents more quickly and configure delivery later. ## Description Fixes an issue where citation chip URLs were incorrectly generated with `undefined` (e.g., `/experiment/amplitude/837199/config/undefined`) when resource IDs lacked an `appId_` prefix. The `getResourceLocationObject` function in `citation-resources.ts` was splitting resource IDs by `_` without checking if enough parts existed. For IDs like `"837199"`, `split('_')` returned `["837199"]`, causing `parts[1]` (the resource ID) to be `undefined`. This change adds `parts.length` checks for EXPERIMENT, FLAG, RELEASE, ANNOTATION, EVENT, CUSTOM_EVENT, and property resource types (and `parts.length > 2` for GROUP_PROPERTY). This ensures `appId` and `id` are correctly extracted, falling back to the original ID and current `appId` from context if the `appId_` prefix is missing, aligning with existing COHORT handler logic. JIRA ticket: ## Testing Manually confirmed the fix by observing correct citation chip URLs in the UI. Ran existing `vitest` tests; all 173 passing tests continued to pass. Verified TypeScript compilation; no new errors were introduced. --- [Slack Thread](https://amplitude.slack.com/archives/C04QGGP1P2S/p1771007223773989?thread_ts=1771007223.773989&cid=C04QGGP1P2S)

 

--- > [!NOTE] > **Low Risk** > Small defensive parsing change limited to citation resource ID splitting; low risk aside from potentially altering URL behavior for malformed/edge-case IDs. > > **Overview** > Fixes citation chip/link URL generation when resource IDs arrive without the expected `appId_` prefix. > > `getResourceLocationObject` now validates `parts.length` before reading split segments for `EVENT`, `CUSTOM_EVENT`, property types, `GROUP_PROPERTY`, and `EXPERIMENT`/`FLAG`/`RELEASE`/`ANNOTATION`, preventing `undefined` IDs and falling back to the original `resource.id`/context appId when the prefix is missing. > > Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit c76f667e29be9d35644a022d3d763d6ce32f3345. ## Description Fixes an issue where the "Heads up we have an outage" butterbar banner was off-center and off-screen to the left. The problem stemmed from the `.butter-bar-group` class in `CoreLayout.module.scss` having `left: 50%; right: 50%`, which created a zero-width fixed container. This caused the butterbar content to overflow and render incorrectly. The fix updates `.butter-bar-group` to span the full viewport width (`left: 0; right: 0`) and adds `pointer-events: none` to prevent the full-width container from blocking clicks on elements below. The butterbar content is correctly centered by its child container's `justify-content: center` property. JIRA ticket: ## Testing Manually verified that the "Heads up we have an outage" butterbar banner now appears correctly centered at the top of the screen and is clickable. --- [Slack Thread](https://amplitude.slack.com/archives/CHKLP0FFC/p1770940225373679?thread_ts=1770940225.373679&cid=CHKLP0FFC)

body:
## Description

Refactored the Global Agent settings UI from a two-step toggle approach (separate visibility and access controls) to a unified three-state toggle group. This simplifies the user experience by consolidating the configuration into a single control with three clear states: Off, Admin preview, and On for everyone.

**Key changes:**
- Replaced two separate `Switch` components with a single `ToggleGroup` component
- Introduced `GlobalAgentState` type and conversion functions (`toTriState`/`fromTriState`) to map between the new three-state model and the underlying `showInSidebar`/`globalAgentEnabled` flags
- Consolidated the two separate toggle handlers into a single `handleStateChange` function that manages both mutations
- Simplified the UI layout by removing the two-step card structure and replacing it with a compact toggle group + description pattern
- Removed unused imports (`Badge`, `BadgeIcon`, `BadgeText`, `Icon`, `Switch`, `cn`)
- Updated toast messages to reflect the new state transitions

The refactoring maintains all existing functionality while providing a cleaner, more intuitive interface for managing Global Agent availability.

## Testing

Tested the following scenarios:
- Toggling between all three states (Off → Admin preview → On for everyone)
- Verifying both API mutations are called when state changes require updates to both `showInSidebar` and `globalAgentEnabled`
- Confirming appropriate toast messages display for each state transition
- Verifying the disabled state is applied during loading and mutation operations
- Testing the "Looking for role-based access controls?" button navigation

No automated tests were added as this is a UI refactor of existing functionality with no logic changes to the underlying mutations or data handling.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01LMq3TJ6JPhyJuV9H2M6VvC

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> UI refactor changes how two backend mutations are coordinated (including deferred save and pending state), which could regress enable/visibility behavior or messaging despite no new backend APIs.
> 
> **Overview**
> Refactors the Global Agent availability controls from two separate switches (visibility + access) into a single three-state `ToggleGroup` (`off` / `admin_preview` / `on`) with an explicit **Save changes** action and clearer state descriptions.
> 
> Adds tri-state mapping helpers and pending-state handling so updates only trigger the necessary mutations (`leftNavVisibility` and/or `globalAgentEnabled`) and consolidates success/error toasts under a single "settings" failure message; updates the settings page copy and i18n strings across `en-US`, `ja-JP`, and `ko-KR` to support the new UI (plus a small Korean translation tweak).
>

body:
## Description

This PR adds a new agents popover button to the Session Replay view header that displays session replay agents for the current project. Users can:
- View a list of recent session replay agents with metadata (updated time, thread count, owner)
- Create new agents directly from the popover
- Navigate to individual agent sessions or the full agents overview

The implementation includes:
- **SessionReplayAgentsPopover** component: A new popover UI that displays agents and provides quick access to agent creation
- **useSessionReplayAgents** hook: Fetches and filters agent sessions by session replay flow types (SESSION_REPLAY_EXPLORER, SESSION_REPLAY_EXPLORER_V2, SESSION_REPLAY_W_OPPORTUNITIES)
- **Lazy loading**: Both the popover and CreateAgentModal are lazy-loaded to avoid bundling heavy agent flows on the session replay page
- **Feature flag gating**: The popover only appears when agents are enabled via the `useIsAgentsEnabled` feature flag

The popover shows:
- Loading state while fetching agents
- Empty state with CTA to create first agent
- List of agents sorted by most recently updated
- Agent metadata including owner name, thread count, and relative update time
- "See all agents" link to navigate to full agents overview

JIRA ticket: <!-- DO NOT MODIFY INSERT: "JIRA" -->

## Testing

- Tested with agents enabled/disabled via feature flag
- Verified popover displays correctly with multiple agents
- Tested empty state when no agents exist
- Verified lazy loading doesn't impact session replay page load performance
- Tested navigation to individual agent sessions and agents overview
- Tested agent creation flow from popover
- Verified loading spinner displays while fetching agents
- Tested with different user ownership scenarios (owned, shared, org-listable agents)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01YNYtg1RXgeVXerbSWYmmhQ

> Adds new session-replay header UI that fetches and displays agent sessions and wires agent creation/invalidation; main risk is regressions in Session Replay header rendering and query invalidation/visibility filtering.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a **feature-flagged “Session Replay Agents” popover** to `SessionReplayView`’s header, showing recent session-replay agents (relative update time, thread count, owner) with links to agent sessions/overview and an inline CTA to create a new agent (lazy-loading both the popover and `CreateAgentModal` to reduce bundle impact).
> 
> Introduces `useSessionReplayAgents` to query and filter agent sessions to session-replay flows, and updates `CreateAgentModal` to (1) support parameterized modal titles (`New {{agentType}}`), (2) enforce flow-params validation before enabling creation, (3) pass `isInModal` through flow-param components for dropdown z-index, and (4) invalidate the same react-query key used by the new session-replay agents list after creation.
>

**This PR refines the Shared Agents UX across multiple touchpoints in the Lightning dashboard, improving discoverability, consistency, and proper permission handling for shared agent access.**

AgentsPopover UI Improvements

Empty State:
• Reduced padding for a more compact layout
• Updated primary CTA to "Create New Agent"

List State:
• Moved "+ New Agent" button to upper right corner of header (small size, tertiary variant)
• Replaced with "See all agents" link at bottom (full width, tertiaryAlt variant for borderless appearance)
• Tightened header and bottom section padding for cleaner look

CreateAgentModal Enhancements

• Changed modal title to "New Dashboard Agent"
• Moved privacy notice ("Agents are private to you...") into modal header
• Reduced spacing throughout header for tighter, more professional layout
• Increased privacy notice text size for better readability
• Added configurable source prop for analytics attribution

Dashboard Subscriptions Modal Integration

• Opens CreateAgentModal directly instead of navigating to separate page
• Lazy loads CreateAgentModal for better bundle performance
• Added tooltip: "Agents can analyze your dashboard and report on top takeaways."
• Passes proper analytics source tracking
• Uses proper i18n for default agent name generation

Access Control for Shared Users

Implemented permission-based UI affordances for users with shared access:

Viewers (shared users) cannot:
• Create new threads (button hidden)
• Add users in ShareAgentModal (search input hidden for non-owners)
• Remove other users from access list
• Edit agent settings (inputs disabled)
• Change general listability settings

Viewers (shared users) can:
• Remove themselves from shared access list
• View agent settings (read-only)
• View all threads
• Copy agent link

Internationalization

Added new translation keys with automatic translations for ja-JP and ko-KR:
• "See all agents"
• "New Dashboard Agent"
• "Close"
• "dashboardSubscriptions.createAgentTooltip"

Bug Fixes

• Fixed Button variant name (tertiaryAlt camelCase)
• Removed unused imports
• Fixed Tooltip component usage
• Restored proper i18n for agent names
• Added explicit analytics source tracking

body:
When users click the "Ask AI" button to open the AI chat sidepanel on chart views, the left chart control container now automatically collapses. This provides more horizontal space for the chart visualization and creates a better user experience.

The implementation listens to the global AI chat state from the @amplitude/ai-chat-core store and triggers the existing collapse functionality when the chat opens.

Added extensive documentation comments for future extension points, including conditional collapse based on screen size, user preferences, chart type, layout mode, and sidebar width.
